# OpenWorkspace

> Zero-dependency TypeScript toolkit for Google Workspace -- CLI, API, MCP Server.

GitHub: https://github.com/ersinkoc/openworkspace
License: MIT | Node.js >= 22 | ESM-only | TypeScript strict

## Install

npm install @openworkspace/core @openworkspace/gmail

## Core Concepts

- Result<T,E> pattern: every function returns `{ ok: true, value: T }` or `{ ok: false, error: E }` -- never throws
- Plugin architecture: each service is a standalone package, registers with the kernel
- HttpClient: authenticated HTTP with request/response/error interceptors
- Auth: OAuth 2.0 (device code flow) and service account JWT

## Packages (19)

@openworkspace/core - kernel, Result type, errors, events, logger, HTTP client, auth, config
@openworkspace/mcp - MCP server, JSON-RPC 2.0, tool registry (28 tools), stdio + HTTP/SSE
@openworkspace/pipeline - YAML workflow engine, expression evaluator, forEach, parallel, retries
@openworkspace/cli - zero-dep CLI parser, 19 command groups
@openworkspace/gmail - search, read, send, labels, threads
@openworkspace/calendar - events, create, free/busy
@openworkspace/drive - search, read, upload, permissions
@openworkspace/sheets - read, write, append, format
@openworkspace/docs - get, create, batch update
@openworkspace/slides - get, create, modify presentations
@openworkspace/contacts - search, list, create
@openworkspace/tasks - list, create, complete, delete
@openworkspace/chat - send messages, manage spaces
@openworkspace/classroom - courses, coursework, students
@openworkspace/forms - get, create, responses
@openworkspace/appscript - run scripts, manage projects
@openworkspace/people - profiles, connections
@openworkspace/groups - list, members, settings
@openworkspace/keep - notes, lists

## API Pattern

All service functions follow this signature:

functionName(http: HttpClient, params) => Promise<Result<T, WorkspaceError>>

Example:

```typescript
import { createHttpClient } from '@openworkspace/core';
import { searchMessages } from '@openworkspace/gmail';

const http = createHttpClient({ baseUrl: 'https://www.googleapis.com', auth: { accessToken: token } });
const result = await searchMessages(http, { query: 'from:boss' });
if (result.ok) console.log(result.value);
else console.error(result.error.message);
```

Result helpers: ok(value), err(error), unwrap(result), unwrapOr(result, fallback), map(result, fn), mapErr(result, fn)

Error types: WorkspaceError, ConfigError, AuthError, NetworkError, RateLimitError, ValidationError, PluginError, NotFoundError

## CLI

Command format: ows <service> <action> [flags]

```
ows auth add user@example.com
ows gmail search "from:boss"
ows gmail send --to alice@example.com --subject "Hi"
ows calendar events --today
ows drive search "quarterly report"
ows drive upload ./file.pdf
ows sheets read SPREADSHEET_ID "Sheet1!A1:D10"
ows tasks list
ows mcp serve --stdio
```

## MCP Server

28 tools for AI assistants. Start with: npx @openworkspace/mcp serve --stdio

Tools: gmail_search, gmail_read, gmail_send, gmail_labels, calendar_events, calendar_create, calendar_freebusy, drive_search, drive_read, drive_upload, sheets_read, sheets_write, tasks_list, tasks_create, tasks_complete, contacts_search, docs_get, docs_create, slides_get, slides_create, classroom_courses, forms_get, chat_send, keep_list, appscript_run, people_me, groups_list, workspace_search

Claude Desktop config:

```json
{ "mcpServers": { "openworkspace": { "command": "npx", "args": ["@openworkspace/mcp", "serve", "--stdio"] } } }
```

## Pipeline

YAML workflow with ${{ expr }} expressions, forEach, parallel, retries.

```yaml
name: example
steps:
  - id: search
    action: gmail.search
    with: { query: "has:attachment newer_than:1d" }
  - id: upload
    action: drive.upload
    forEach: ${{ steps.search.result }}
```

## Development

pnpm install && pnpm build && pnpm test
